meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Series of tremors near Tehran renews concerns over major quake risk
items.summary: The tremors have been felt in an area close to the Mosha fault, one of Iran’s most active seismic zones.

items.title: UN resolution calls for freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: UN resolution calls for freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Jason Collins, first openly gay active NBA player, dies after cancer battle
items.summary: The 213cm centre, who publicly came out as gay in 2013, had been battling a brain tumour.

items.title: How Silicon Valley giants are turning into war contractors
items.summary: Palantir, Anduril, Google and other tech giants are selling AI-powered, computer-guided weapons systems.

items.title: Messi doubles MLS base salary to make $28m a year at Inter Miami
items.summary: Miami and Argentina player earns more than twice as much as the second-highest-paid player in Major League Soccer.

items.title: China’s Xi expected to press Trump on Taiwan, tariffs during summit
items.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping likely to seek concessions on arms sales to Taiwan, analysts say.

items.title: Iran war day 75: Trump-Xi talks loom in Beijing as tensions rise in Gulf
items.summary: Trump heads to Beijing for high-stakes talks with Xi Jinping as tensions over the Iran war continue to escalate.

items.title: Wembanyama returns, Spurs rout Wolves for 3-2 lead in NBA Playoff series
items.summary: Wembanyama scores 27 points on return from ejection as San Antonio Spurs beat Minnesota Timberwolves 126-97 in Game 5.

items.title: UK PM Starmer meets rival Streeting amid pledge to carry on governing
items.summary: UK PM, who is battling to remain in his job amid the resignations of ministers, held brief talks his health secretary.

items.title: Trump and Xi to meet in Beijing: The key issues shaping the China summit
items.summary: Trump and Xi meet as trade, Taiwan and the Iran war dominate a high-stakes US-China summit.

items.title: Tens of thousands protest in Argentina over Milei university cuts
items.summary: Argentines protest Javier Milei’s deep cuts to tuition-free universities as staff wages plunge and funding law stalls.

items.title: US-China head-to-head: Explained in 11 maps and charts
items.summary: Before President Trump’s visit to Beijing, Al Jazeera compares the US and China on economics, military and resources.

items.title: Ronaldo’s Al-Nassr kept waiting for Saudi Pro League title by own goal
items.summary: Goalkeeper Bento's bizarre injury-time own goal denies Al-Nassr a title-crowning 1-0 win over rivals Al Hilal at home.

items.title: Bahrain-led UN resolution on Strait of Hormuz gains support of 112 nations
items.summary: The resolution calls for free navigation through the strait and an end to Iranian attacks on its Gulf neighbours.

items.title: Fighting in Sudan’s Blue Nile State displaces thousands
items.summary: Al Jazeera’s Hiba Morgan reports from Al Karama camp in Al-Damazin, Blue Nile State, southeastern Sudan.

items.title: Mexico, CIA reject report of US assassination campaign against cartels
items.summary: Rebuttals come after US media report alleges that the CIA has 'directly participated' in deadly anti-cartel operations.

items.title: Zelenskyy’s ex-chief of staff appears in court in money-laundering case
items.summary: Andriy Yermak is suspected in an alleged $10.5m money-laundering scheme involving a luxury real estate development.

items.title: Putin hails Russia’s test launch of ‘most powerful missile in the world’
items.summary: Russian leader says the nuclear-capable Sarmat missile will enter combat service at the end of the year.

items.title: ‘It’s a failed nation’: Trump pressures Cuba as fuel crisis deepens
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has called Cuba ‘a failed nation’, as his administration expands its pressure campaign.

items.title: Argentines protest against Milei’s defunding of public universities
items.summary: Argentines protest against Milei’s defunding of public universities.

items.title: FBI Director Kash Patel fires back at drinking allegations
items.summary: FBI Director Kash Patel and Senator Chris Van Hollen had a heated exchange during a Senate budget hearing.

items.title: Peru presidential candidate Roberto Sanchez charged with financial crimes
items.summary: Prosecutor calls for leftist candidate to be jailed for five years and four months over false financial disclosures.

items.title: Starmer at risk because he pushed Labour to be ‘new Conservative Party’
items.summary: Author Oliver Eagleton explains why British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is fighting for his job.

items.title: Iran war live: World watches as Trump and Xi to hold high-stakes talks
items.summary: US president says he 'doesn’t think about Americans’ financial situation' when it comes to the crippling war on Iran.

items.title: Chinese firm unveils ‘transformer’ style manned robot
items.summary: Unitree Robotics has released footage of its CEO piloting the GD01 transformable mecha that smashes through walls.
